name: Repair Barge

src.description: Once per turn, just after an enemy unit finishes making its attacks, if one or more friendly NECRON WARRIORS units within 3"of this model lost one or more wounds as a result of those attacks, this model can use this ability. If it does, select one of those NECRON WARRIORS units; that unit’s Reanimation Protocols activate. The same NECRON WARRIORS unit cannot be selected for this ability more than once per turn.

note: Too vague ("once per round special"). Missing: Reanimation Protocols trigger, target specificity, one-use-per-unit limit.

name: Titanic Walker

src.description: Each time this model makes a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, it can move over models (excluding TITANIC models) and terrain features that are 4"or less in height as if they were not there.

note: Missing: models and terrain height limit (≤4"); excludes TITANIC models.

name: Relentless Combatants

src.description: You can re-roll Charge rolls made for this unit, and this unit is eligible to declare a charge in a turn in which it Fell Back.

note: Missing: eligible to charge after Falling Back (not just re-rolling).

name: Their Number is Legion

src.description: Each time this unit’s Reanimation Protocols activate, you can re-roll the dice to see how many wounds are reanimated.

note: "Modify stats" vague/wrong. Actual rule: re-roll wounds reanimated on Reanimation activation.

name: Harassment Swarm (Aura)

src.description: While an enemy unit (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES) is within 3"of this unit, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.

note: Aura range wrong (3", not 6"). Excludes MONSTERS/VEHICLES from aura.

name: Nanoscarab Projector

src.description: Once per battle round, when a friendly NECRONS unit within 3"of this model’s Reanimation Protocols activate, it can use this ability. If it does, that unit reanimates 1 additional wound.

note: Scope says "aura 6" but rule is once-per-round, targets friendly NECRONS within 3".

name: Accelerator Mandible

src.description: At the start of the Fight phase, select one friendly CANOPTEK unit within 3"of this unit. Until the end of the phase, improve the Weapon Skill characteristic of models in that unit by 1.

note: "Modify stats" vague. Specific: improves Weapon Skill +1 for friendly CANOPTEK within 3" at Fight start.

name: Technomancer

src.description: At the end of your Movement phase, you can select one friendly NECRONS model within 6"of the bearer. That model regains up to D3 lost wounds. Each model can only be selected for this ability once per turn.

note: Scope says aura/permanent but triggers at Movement phase end, one-use-per-turn, within 6" only.

name: Quantum Invader

src.description: This model can be set up in the Reinforcements step of your first, second or third Movement phase, regardless of any mission rules.

note: Nonsensical text (repeated clauses). Actual rule: can be set up from Reserves in turns 1-3.

name: Invasion Beams

src.description: At the end of the Fight phase, if there are no models currently embarked within this TRANSPORT, you can select one friendly NECRONS INFANTRY unit wholly within 6"of this TRANSPORT. Unless that unit is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, it can embark within this TRANSPORT.

note: "Special embark rule" vague. Details: at Fight phase end, selects unit within 6", must not be in engagement range.

name: My Will Be Done

src.description: Once per battle round, one unit from your army with this ability can use it when it is targeted with a Stratagem. If it does, reduce the CP cost of that usage of that Stratagem by 1CP.

note: Scope says one-use but applies once per battle round. Scope missing: applies to targeted Stratagem.

name: Resurrection Orb

src.description: Once per battle, while the bearer is leading a unit, at the end of any phase, it can resurrect that unit if it is on the battlefield. When you do, that unit’s Reanimation Protocols are activated, reanimating D6 wounds rather than D3 when doing so. You cannot resurrect more than one unit per turn.

note: "Return 1 model" incomplete. Actually: reanimates D6 wounds (vs. D3 normally), once per battle, one-unit-per-turn limit.

name: Engrammatic Logic

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of any phase, you can select one friendly NECRONS unit that is Battle-shocked and within 12"of this model. That unit is no longer Battle-shocked.

note: Scope says "one use" and "aura 12" but applies once per battle. Distance correct.

name: Sentinel Construct

src.description: Each time you target this unit with the Fire Overwatch Stratagem, while resolving that Stratagem, hits are scored on unmodified Hit rolls of 5+.

note: Overwatch (Fire Overwatch Stratagem); hits on 5+ not "-5 to hit". Modifier direction/value mischaracterized.

name: Implacable Eradication

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, re-roll a Wound roll of 1. If the target of that attack is within range of an objective marker, you can re-roll the Wound roll instead.

note: Missing: conditional re-roll (all wounds if target near objective; ones only otherwise).

name: Bound Creation

src.description: While this unit is in the same unit as a CRYPTEK model, that CRYPTEK model has the Feel No Pain 4+ ability.

note: Scope/target wrong: applies to CRYPTEK in same unit as CRYPTOTHRALLS, not unit-wide.

name: Systematic Vigour

src.description: Each time a CRYPTOTHRALL model in this unit is destroyed by a melee attack, if that model has not fought this phase, roll one D6:on a 2+, do not remove it from play. The destroyed model can fight after the attacking model’s unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.

note: Applies to CRYPTOTHRALL, melee-only, DC roll 2+. Scope says unit-wide.

name: Cryptek Retinue

src.description: At the start of the Declare Battle Formations step, this unit can join one other unit from your army that is being led by a CRYPTEK model (a unit cannot have more than one CRYPTOTHRALLS unit joined to it). If it does, until the end of the battle, every model in this unit counts as being part of that Bodyguard unit, and that Bodyguard unit’s Starting Strength is increased accordingly.

note: "Forced attachment" vague. Actual: joins led Bodyguard unit at Declare Formations; one-per-unit, increases Starting Strength.

name: Master Chronomancer

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, models in that unit have a 4+ invulnerable save.

note: Placeholder "?+" missing value (4+). Applies while leading.

name: The Stars Are Right

src.description: Once per battle, at the start of the Fight phase, this model can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the phase, triple the Attacks and Strength characteristics of this model’s Staff of Tomorrow and every successful Wound roll made for this model’s attacks scores a Critical Wound.

note: Text inverted/corrupted. Actual: triples Attacks and Strength (not negatives), Staff of Tomorrow only, once per battle.

name: Powers of the C’tan

src.description: In your Shooting phase, when this model is selected to shoot, first select up to two different C’tan Powers weapons. Until the end of the phase, this model is equipped with those weapons in addition to its other weapons (this model cannot make attacks with any other C’tan Powers weapon you did not select in this way this phase).

note: Garbled syntax. Core mechanic: select up to two C'tan Powers weapons per Shooting phase, can't use others selected.

name: Evasion Engrams

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this unit has shot, it can make a Normal move of up to 6". If it does, until the end of the turn, this unit is not eligible to declare a charge.

note: Post-shoot move up to 6"; becomes ineligible to charge. "Shoot and scoot" label imprecise.

name: Shieldvanes

src.description: The bearer has a Save characteristic of 3+ and a Move characteristic of 8".

note: Also sets Move to 8"; generated omits movement bonus.

name: Nebuloscope

src.description: Ranged weapons equipped by the bearer have the [IGNORES COVER] ability.

note: Applies to ranged weapons only ("Ignores Cover"), not all weapons.

name: Shadowloom

src.description: The bearer has the Stealth ability.

note: Generated: "weapons gain Stealth". Actual: bearer has Stealth (ability, not weapon).

name: Nightmare Shroud (Aura)

src.description: In the Battle-shock step of your opponent’s Command phase, if an enemy unit that is below its Starting Strength is within 6"of this model, that enemy unit must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 1 from the test when it does so.

note: Applies to enemy below Starting Strength; happens in opponent's Command phase (Battle-shock step).

name: Harbinger of Despair

src.description: Once per turn, at the start of your Command, Movement, Shooting, Charge or Fight phase, you can select one enemy unit within 18"of this model. That unit must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 1 from the test when it does so.

note: Once per turn, any phase start (not Fight-only). Subtracts 1 from test.

name: Optimised for Slaughter

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack with an enmitic exterminator that targets a unit (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES), re-roll a Wound roll of 1. Each time a model in this unit makes an attack with a gauss destructor that targets a MONSTER or VEHICLE, re-roll a Wound roll of 1.

note: "If target has A" vague. Actually: weapon-specific (enmitic exterminator vs. gauss destructor) and target-type specific.

name: Whirling Onslaught

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a melee attack, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. If this unit made a Charge move this turn, you can re-roll the Hit roll instead.

note: Unconditional re-roll ones; full re-roll if charged this turn (not conditional on charged turn).

name: Plasmacyte

src.description: Once per battle for each Plasmacyte this unit has, when this unit is selected to fight, you can use this ability. If you do, until the end of the phase, melee weapons equipped by models in this unit have the [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] ability.

note: Once per battle per Plasmacyte; melee weapons only. Scope says "phase" but applies once per item.

name: Reanimation Nodes (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS INFANTRY unit is within 6"of this FORTIFICATION, models in that unit have the Feel No Pain 6+ ability.

note: Aura applies to NECRONS INFANTRY only (not all units). Within 6" is correct.

name: Ancient Cover

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this FORTIFICATION, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Scope says "permanent" but triggered contextually (line of sight). Applies to ranged attacks on this fortification.

name: Fortification

src.description:
While an enemy unit is only within Engagement Range of one or more FORTIFICATIONS from your army:

■ That unit can still be selected as the target of ranged attacks, but each time such an attack is made, unless it is made with a Pistol, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.
■ Models in that unit do not need to take Desperate Escape tests due to Falling Back while Battle-shocked, except for those that will move over enemy models when doing so.

note: Covers -1 to hit (non-Pistol only). Omits: Desperate Escape relief for Falling Back while Battle-shocked.

name: Deployment

src.description: When this unit is first set up on the battlefield, its models do not have to be set up in Unit Coherency. Instead, each model must be set up wholly within 12"of one other model from its unit. From that point on, each model in this unit is treated as a separate unit.

note: Models treated as separate units afterward (critical detail). Coherency setup rule correct.

name: United In Destruction

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, melee weapons equipped by models in that unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Melee weapons only (not all weapons). Omits leadership context.

name: Crimson Harvest

src.description: Each time this model ends a Charge move, select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of this model and roll one D6:on a 2-5, that unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 6, that unit suffers D3+3 mortal wounds.

note: Missing DC: 2-5 = D3, 6 = D3+3. Generated doesn't differentiate roll outcomes.

name: Hyperspace Hunters

src.description: Once per turn, in the Reinforcements step of your opponent’s Movement phase, when an enemy unit is set up on the battlefield from Reserves within 18"of and visible to this unit, this unit can shoot as if it were your Shooting phase, but must only target that enemy unit when doing so, and can only do so if that enemy unit is an eligible target.

note: "Once per round special" vague. Actual: shoots at Reserves entry in opponent's Movement phase, once per turn.

name: Targeting Relay

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks. Until the end of the phase, that unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover.

note: Denies Benefit of Cover to hit unit (not grants it). Scope/effect inverted.

name: Lord of the Storm

src.description: Once per battle, at the end of your Command phase, this model can use this ability. If it does, roll one D6 for each enemy unit within 12"of this model:on a 2-5, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 6, that enemy unit suffers D3+3 mortal wounds.

note: Missing DC: 2-5 = D3, 6 = D3+3. Triggers at Command phase end, once per battle.

name: Gravitic Pulse

src.description: At the start of your opponent’s Movement phase, you can select one enemy unit within 18"of and visible to this model. Until the end of the turn, halve the Move characteristic of models in that unit and halve Advance and Charge rolls made for that unit. In addition, if that unit can FLY, until the start of your next Movement phase, roll one D6 each time that unit ends any type of move:on a 4+, that unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.

note: "Deal mortal wounds" incomplete. Core: halves Move/rolls; FLY units roll for D3 MW at move end.

name: Nanoscarab Reanimation Beam (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS unit is within 3"of this model, each time that unit’s Reanimation Protocols activate, that unit reanimates an additional D3 wounds.

note: Vague 'modify stats' misses the actual reanimation mechanic (D3 wounds heal).

name: Advanced Quantum Shielding

src.description: Each time an attack targets this model, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than this model’s Toughness characteristic, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.

note: Missing: only applies when Strength > Toughness condition.

name: Hard-wired for Destruction

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets the closest eligible target, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. If the target of that attack is within range of an objective marker your opponent controls, you can re-roll the Hit roll instead.

note: Misses core mechanic (closest eligible target trigger and secondary condition with objective control).

name: Guardian Protocols

src.description: While a NOBLE model is leading this unit, each time an attack targets this unit, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than the Toughness characteristic of this unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.

note: Missing: only applies when Strength > Toughness condition.

name: Dispersion Shield

src.description: The bearer has a 4+ invulnerable save.

note: Unknown invulnerable save value; should be 4+.

name: Eternity Gate

src.description: In the Reinforcements step of your Movement phase, you can select one NECRONS INFANTRY unit from your army that is either in Reserves or on the battlefield (if you select the latter, remove that unit from the battlefield and place it into Reserves). That unit is then set up anywhere on the battlefield that is wholly within 6"of this model and not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units. That unit cannot declare a charge this turn.

note: Oversimplified; misses unit selection rules, reserves mechanics, and engagement range exclusion.

name: Translocation Shroud

src.description: Each time this model’s unit Advances, do not make an Advance roll for it. Instead, until the end of the phase, add 6"to the Move characteristic of models in that unit. In addition, each time a model in that unit makes a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, until that move is finished, it can move horizontally through models and terrain features (it cannot finish a move on top of another model or its base).

note: Vastly incomplete; omits no-roll advance, terrain/model phasing, and ending position rules.

name: Self-destruction

src.description: At the start of the Fight phase, if this unit is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, you can select one model in this unit to destroy. If you do, select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of that model and roll one D6, adding 1 to the result if that unit is a VEHICLE. On a 2-5, that unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 6+, that unit suffers 3 mortal wounds.

note: Missing: only triggers at Fight phase start, requires enemy in Engagement Range, damage scales (D3 or 3).

name: Chittering Swarm

src.description: While an enemy unit is within Engagement Range of this unit, subtract 1 from the Objective Control characteristic of models in that enemy unit (to a minimum of 1). While this unit is within 6"of one or more friendly CRYPTEK models, the Objective Control characteristic of models in this unit is 1.

note: Correct outline but syntactically confusing notation; OC reduction has minimum of 1.

name: Wraith Form

src.description: Each time this unit ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move and roll one D6 for each model in this unit:for each 4+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Missing: only after Normal move, only enemies moved over, roll per model.

name: Ancient Collector

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, at the end of your Command phase, if this unit is within range of an objective marker you control, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase.

note: Vague OC modification misses actual mechanic (objective control duration extension).

name: Surrogate Hosts

src.description: At the start of your Command phase, if this model is on the battlefield, you can select one other friendly NECRONS INFANTRY CHARACTER model on the battlefield (excluding SKORPEKH LORD and EPIC HERO models). The selected model is destroyed (ignoring any rules that are triggered when a model is destroyed) and this model is put in its place, with all of its wounds remaining (if the selected model was leading a unit, this model now attaches to that unit as its Leader).

note: Describes swapping with another model; text is too vague to parse the actual mechanics.

name: Destroyer Cult

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes a ranged attack, a successful unmodifed Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.

note: Describes -5 to hits (wrong direction); actually grants Critical Hits on 5+ unmodified.

name: Stealth

src.description: If every model in a unit has this ability, then each time a ranged attack is made against it, subtract 1 from that attack's Hit roll.

note: Missing: requires every model in unit to have ability.

name: Flesh Hunger

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a melee attack, if the target of that attack is Below Half-strength, a successful Hit roll scores a Critical Hit.

note: Wrong mechanic: not -1 to hits, rather Critical Hits against Below Half-strength targets.

name: Malevolent Arcing

src.description: In your Shooting phase, each time you select a target for this model’s twin tesla destructor, roll one D6 for the target unit and one D6 for every other enemy unit within 3"of the target unit. On a 5+, the unit being rolled for is struck by arcing energies; after resolving all of this model’s attacks against the target unit, each unit struck by arcing energies suffers D3 mortal wounds.

note: Oversimplified; misses twin tesla destructor requirement, 3" arc, and selective targeting.

name: Illuminor

src.description: While this model is within 3"of one or more other friendly NECRONS units, this model has the Lone Operative ability.

note: Triggers when within 3" of other friendly NECRONS units, not aura granting.

name: Mechanical Augmentation (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS BATTLELINE unit is within 3"of this model, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1, and each time an attack targets that unit, worsen the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.

note: Correct idea but scope unclear; misses bidirectional AP mods (friendly +1, enemy -1).

name: Atomic Energy Manipulator

src.description: At the end of the Fight phase, if this model destroyed one or more models this phase, until the end of the battle, add 3"to the range of its Mechanical Augmentation ability (to a maximum of 12").

note: Vague 'range extension'; should specify +3" to Mechanical Augmentation aura range.

name: Fights First

src.description: Units with this ability that are eligible to fight do so in the Fights First step, provided every model in the unit has this ability.

note: Missing: requirement that every model in unit has ability.

name: Protective Disciples

src.description: While this model is within 3"of one or more other friendly DESTROYER CULT units, this model has the Lone Operative ability.

note: Triggers within 3" of DESTROYER CULT units specifically, not general aura mechanic.

name: Infectious Murder-Madness (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS unit (excluding MONSTER and TITANIC units) is within 6” of this model, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, if that model has the DESTROYER CULT keyword or that enemy unit is the closest eligible target, that attack has the [SUSTAINED HITS 1] ability.

note: Misses conditions: requires DESTROYER CULT keyword or closest enemy target.

name: Prophet of Destruction

src.description: Each time this model destroys an enemy unit, select one other friendly DESTROYER CULT unit within 9” of this model. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, re-roll a Wound roll of 1.

note: Scope issue: affects selected friendly unit within 9", not general unit rerolls.

name: Nullstone Field Generator (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS unit is within 6"of the bearer, models in that unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability against mortal wounds and Psychic Attacks.

note: Correct Feel No Pain but missing: only against mortal wounds and Psychic Attacks.

name: Weapon Sentinels

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets a unit within 12",you can ignore any or all modifiers to the following:that attack’s Ballistic Skill characteristic; the Hit roll; the Wound roll

note: Missing conditions: only within 12", only ranged attacks; references undefined "A" attribute.

name: Canoptek Retinue

src.description: At the start of the Declare Battle Formations step, this unit can join one other unit from your army that is being led by a CRYPTEK model (a unit cannot have more than one Tomb Crawlers unit joined to it and cannot have both a TOMB CRAWLERS and a CRYPTOTHRALLS unit joined to it). If it does, until the end of the battle, every model in this unit counts as being part of that Bodyguard unit, and that Bodyguard unit’s Starting Strength is increased accordingly.

note: Vague 'forced attachment'; actual mechanic is joining as bodyguard with composition rules.

name: Tunnelling Horrors

src.description: At the end of your opponent’s turn, if this unit is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, you can remove this unit from the battlefield. In the Reinforcements step of your next Movement phase, set it up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9"horizontally away from all enemy models.

note: Correct outline but misses 9" horizontal distance from enemy models requirement.

name: Harbinger of Destruction

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes a ranged attack, a successful unmodifed Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.

note: Describes -5 to hits (wrong); actually grants Critical Hits on 5+ unmodified.

name: Living Lightning

src.description: In your Shooting phase, select one enemy unit within 18"of and visible to this model (excluding units with the Lone Operative ability that are not part of an Attached unit and are not within 12"of this model) and roll four D6:for each 4+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Oversimplified; misses visibility requirement, Lone Operative exception, and 4D6 roll per target.

name: Overwhelming Obliteration

src.description: In your Movement phase, if this model Remains Stationary, until the end of the turn, its doomsday cannon has the [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] ability.

note: Correct general idea but loses specificity: only doomsday cannon, only when Remains Stationary.

name: Inescapable Death

src.description: Once per turn, one unit from your army with this ability can be targeted with the Fire Overwatch Stratagem for 0CP, even if you have already used that Stratagem on a different unit this phase. In addition, each time you target this unit with the Fire Overwatch Stratagem, while resolving that Stratagem, hits are scored on unmodified Hit rolls of 2+.

note: Vague CP refund and special rules; misses Fire Overwatch stratagem interaction and 2+ hit bonus.

name: Multi-threat Eliminator

src.description: Once per turn, in your opponent’s Shooting phase, when an enemy unit makes a ranged attack that targets a friendly NECRONS unit within 3"of a model with this ability, after that enemy unit has shot, one model with this ability that is within 3"of that target can shoot as if it were your Shooting phase, but it must target only that enemy unit when doing so, and can only do so if that enemy unit is an eligible target.

note: Vague 'once per round special'; actually allows reactive return fire in opponent's phase.

name: Atavistic Instigation

src.description:
Each time this model targets an enemy unit with its heavy death ray, your opponent must declare if that unit will stand firm or duck for cover:

■ If it stands firm, when resolving ranged attacks against that unit this phase, a successful unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit. 

■ If it ducks for cover, until the start of your next Shooting phase, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.

note: Wrong mechanic: misses enemy choice (stand firm or duck), Critical Hit trigger, and -1 hit penalty scope.

name: Tectonic Reverberations

src.description: In your Movement phase, you can select one enemy unit within 18"of and visible to this model. Until the start of your next Movement phase that enemy unit is pinned. While a unit is pinned, subtract 2 from that unit’s Move characteristic and subtract 2 from Charge rolls made for it.

note: Vague 'modify stats'; actually pins enemies (Move -2, Charge -2) within 18".

name: Obelisk Node Control

src.description: While this model is within range of an objective marker you control, enemy units that are set up on the battlefield from Reserves cannot be set up within 12"of this model.

note: Correct but requires clarification: exclusion zone only applies to friendly-controlled objectives.

name: Vanguard Protocols

src.description: If this model is attached to a CANOPTEK MACROCYTES unit during the Declare Battle Formations step, this model has the Scouts 8"ability.

note: Vague 'attached unit eligibility'; actually grants Scouts 8" conditionally to CANOPTEK MACROCYTES.

name: Triarchal Menhirs

src.description: If this unit’s Szarekh model is destroyed, all of this unit’s remaining Triarchal Menhir models are also destroyed.

note: Vague destruction rule; only non-leader Menhirs destroyed when Szarekh dies.

name: Chronometron

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model’s unit has shot, if it is not within Engagement Range of any enemy units, that unit can make a Normal move of up to 5". If it does, until the end of the turn, that unit is not eligible to declare a charge.

note: Correct outline but missing: only after unit has shot, only 5" move, no charge until end of turn.

name: Canoptek Swarm

src.description: In your Command phase, select one friendly CANOPTEK SCARAB SWARM unit within 6"of this unit. One destroyed model is returned to that CANOPTEK SCARAB SWARM unit for each SPYDER model in this unit.

note: Condition clarity poor; mechanics off (one model per Spyder, selected unit within 6").

name: Gloom Prism (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly NECRONS unit is within 6"of the bearer, models in that unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability against mortal wounds and Psychic Attacks.

note: Correct Feel No Pain but missing: only against mortal wounds and Psychic Attacks.

name: Phased Cover

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this FORTIFICATION, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Scope issue: only applies against attacks via this FORTIFICATION, not unit in general.

name: Ziggurat Dock

src.description: One friendly MONOLITH, OBELISK, TESSERACT VAULT or SENTRY PYLON model can start the battle on top of this FORTIFICATION’s ziggurat platform. While such a model is on this platform, that model’s ranged weapons have the [HEAVY] ability. Any friendly model can be set up or end any type of move on the ramps or platform sections of this FORTIFICATION.

note: Oversimplified; misses specific allowed models, platform mechanic, and ramp placement rules.

name: Phase-shifted Cover

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this FORTIFICATION, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Scope issue: only applies against attacks via this FORTIFICATION, not unit in general.

name: Teleportation Matrix

src.description: Once per battle, at the end of your opponent’s turn, if this FORTIFICATION is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units, you can remove it from the battlefield. In the Reinforcements step of your next Movement phase, set it up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy models. If the battle ends and this FORTIFICATION is not on the battlefield, it is destroyed.

note: Vague 'once per battle special'; misses end-of-opponent-turn timing and destruction if not deployed.

name: Death Sphere Bombardment

src.description: Each time this model ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move and roll six D6: for each 3+, that unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Misses condition: only after Normal move, roll 6D6 (not just D6), threshold is 3+.

name: Phase Shift Generator (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly Necrons unit is within 3" of this Fortification, models in that unit have a 5+ invulnerable save against ranged attacks.

note: Missing range condition (3") and attack type (ranged only). Aura is 6 but mechanics apply to units within 3".

name: Mind in the Machine

src.description: At the start of your opponent’s Shooting phase, select one enemy Vehicle unit within 12" of and visible to this model. That unit must take a Leadership test. If that test is passed, until the end of the phase, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll; if that test is failed, that unit is not eligible to shoot this phase.

note: Omits leadership test requirement; missing failure condition (suppresses shooting). Completely misses core mechanic.

name: The Vargard’s Duty

src.description: While this model is in the same unit as Nemesor Zahndrekh, Character models in that unit have the Feel No Pain 4+ ability.

note: Omits restriction to Character models and squad condition (must be in same unit as named leader).

name: Counter-tactics

src.description: Once per battle, after your opponent uses a Stratagem, if this model is on the battlefield, it can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the battle, the CP cost your opponent must pay to use that Stratagem again is increased by 1CP.

note: Generated text incomprehensible; actual ability increases opponent's CP cost, not grants CP to player.

name: Transient Madness

src.description:
While this model is leading a unit, at the start of your Command phase, roll one D6: until the start of your next Command phase, weapons equipped by models in that unit gain the ability below that corresponds with that roll:
■ 1-2: [SUSTAINED HITS 1]
■ 3-4: [LETHAL HITS]
■ 5-6: [DEVASTATING WOUNDS]

note: Generated lists all three abilities as simultaneous; actually random roll picks one per command phase.

name: Reanimation Protocols
note: Setting W to 3 is opposite of actual mechanic (healing wounds D3 at a time with specific priority rules).

name: Matter Absorption

src.description: At the start of your Shooting phase, select one enemy VEHICLE unit within 12"of this model and roll one D6:on a 2+, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds and this model regains up to that many lost wounds.

note: Generated text too vague; actual ability triggers at shooting phase start on Vehicle, deals mortal wounds and heals bearer.

name: Grand Illusion

src.description: If your army includes this model, after both players have deployed their armies, select up to three NECRONS units from your army and redeploy them. When doing so, you can set those units up in Strategic Reserves if you wish, regardless of how many units are already in Strategic Reserves.

note: Generated text too vague; actual ability enables post-deployment redeploy and reserves setup of three units.

name: Transdimensional Displacement

src.description: Each time this model is selected to Advance, you can remove it from the battlefield and set it up again anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9"horizontally away from all enemy units.

note: Condition misplaced: triggered on Advance action, not solely when advanced before turn. Scope/range condition vague.
